sutta: once the verbal sariputta and the verbal maha mogalana with the company of the monks of the sangha walked a tour near south hill and it was then that nanda's mother a lay disciple of beilukanda rose before dawn 1 night and sang the way to the beyond now at that time the royal deva where savannah was going from the north to the southern quarter on some business or other and he heard nanda's mother singing and stopped and waited for the end and when she had finished she was silent then the royal deva besavana perceiving she had finished rejoiced greatly saying well done sister well done and she said but who is it o tau of august countenance sister it is i your brother the royal deva away savannah and she said hail august 1 pray let this dhamma him sung by me be your gift of greeting and he said well done sister yes let this verily be my gift of greeting tomorrow monks of the sangha with sarikuta and moghalana at their head will come to vailukanda not having had the morning meal when you have fed them you should declare the offering to be mine and it shall be my gift of greeting and when the night was over nanda's mother had much hard and soft food prepared in her house and the mouths of the sangha with sarikuta and moghalana at the head arrived at veylukanda having had no morning meal hernandez mother called the man and said go my good man to the monk's park and say it is time raven sirs the meal is ready in the lady nanda's mother's house yes lady he replied and he did so and dressing early taking bowl and rope the mountains with sarikuta and mogulana at their head came to nanda's mother's house and sat down on the seats arranged there and the lay disciple nanda's mother served them with her own hands with much hard and soft food and satisfied them and when the verbal sarikuta had eaten and withdrawn his hand from the bowl nanda's mother sat down at 1 side and he said to her seated thus but who told you o mother of nanda of the coming of the monks of the sangha and she said proven sir i arose in the night before dawn i sang the way to the beyond and was island then came the royal day by way savannah and told me of your arrival and begged me to declare this offering his reference let all the merit in this giving be to the happiness of the royal deva vasavana it is marvelous and wonderful oh mother of nanda that you should talk face to face with a deva so powerful so mighty i'll just stop here for a moment to comment you see this nanda's mother sang the way to the beyond it is 1 of the sutas in the sutani pata and sutani pata now is considered 1 of the old overall sutasla and this deva came to congratulate her and she could talk with this deva that means this nanda's mother is quite an accomplished lay disciple he could see the deva and could talk with the deva and the deva told her to make this food offering to the monks and say that the offering is his so nanda's mother offered the monks the food and after the monks had eaten their food is set here and when the venerable sariputta had eaten and withdrawn his hand from the bowl nanda's mother sat down at 1 side so you see normally if the monks have not finished eating now they would still hold on to the bowl but when they are finished eating they withdraw the hand from the bowl properly they put down the bowl then the lay people can come and talk to the monks because in the vinaya of the monks unless all the monks have finished eating they don't get up they wait for everyone to have finished eating so so this um so this nanda's mother said to the variable sariputta here let all the merit in this giving be to the happiness of the royal deva vesavana as this is an instance of the transference of merit this person nanda's mother had given this food offering and she said now this is all the merit in all this giving is for the happiness of the royal deva savannah in the 4 nikayas we have only 2 sutas concerning the transference of merit this is 1 of them the other 1 is in anguttara nikaya 10.177 concerning merit for the dead [Music] and then the suta continues when this nanda's mother said probably sir that is not the only marvelous thing that has happened to me there is indeed another rajas for some reason took by force and slew my only son nanda who was dear and precious to me yet when the boy was seized or being seized bound or being bound slain or being slain i knew no disturbance of heart and verbal sarikuta said it is wonderful it is marvelous for a mother of nanda that you should have so perched the surges of the heart of the disturbances of the heart and she said no is that all raven sir when my husband died he arose among the yakas and he revealed himself to me in his old form but i knew no disturbance of heart on that account again nervous sarkuta said it is marvelous or mother of nanda and she said now is that all raven sir from the day i a young girl was brought to my husband then but a youth i know of no trespass even in thought against my husband how then indeed it is marvelous for mother of nanda that you should have so perched the surges of the heart now is that all reverend sir from the day i became a confessed lay disciple i know of no intentional transgression of any step of the training it is marvelous for mother of nanda it is wonderful now is that all reverend sir for just as long as i wish being aloof from sen's desires aloof from unwholesome states i enter and abide in the first jhana which is accompanied by applied and sustained thought with delight and pleasure born of solitude stealing such thoughts i enter and abide in the second jana which has internal self-confidence and singleness of mind without applied and sustained thought but with delight and pleasure born of concentration then with the fading away of delight i enter and abide in the third jana i abide equanimous mindful aware and i experience the pleasure of body where of aryans declare the economist and mindful dwells pleasurably then by putting away pleasure by putting away pain by the passing away of former joy and grief of mind i enter and abide in the fourth jana a complete purity of mindfulness and equanimity which is neither pleasurable nor painful and verbal sariputta said it is marvelous or mother of nanda it is wonderful problem sir that is not all there is yet another marvelous thing those 5 lower factors declared by the exalted 1 i perceive not 1 in myself unabandoned it is marvellous wonderful mother of nanda then the venerable sariputta instructed nanda's mother with dharmatov incited her inspired her gladden her and rising from his seat he departed as the end of the sutta
commentary: so you see this nanda's mother is a very rare disciple of the buddha a very gem of a disciple with so many wonderful qualities and when the sun was slain the only son or so she did not grief and she can attain all the 4 janas and she has abandoned the 5 lower factors in other words she is a third stage third russian arya you notice in the description of the 4 jaanas in the fourth jana it is said to be a complete purity of mindfulness and equanimity uh the states of chana are states of intense mindfulness and the next utah is
